Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

I heard SP said that chanting 16 rounds & following the 4 regs is sufficient to go back to Godhead

Sadhana · asked by user [] · 2007-09-06 · 12 answers
Anyone hear about this statement?
user [147] · 2007-09-07
Yes.
user [19] · 2007-09-09
and dont commit offences
user [296] · 2008-06-07
it is, but there is a long purport to it
user [166] · 2008-06-07
Please present the purport premasru....it would make very interesting reading.
user [296] · 2008-06-08
Im sorry rasa108, I dont have a purport as such, but I would like to research it and come back. Personally, I would say it means chanting the holy name without offense as abhiram said, so it is not so simple as it sounds. There is always a deeper purport to sadhus sayings.
user [154] · 2008-06-08
Prabhupada would often chant 12 rounds. Sometimes he would chant more.
user [166] · 2008-06-08
[quote][cite] ccd:[/cite]Prabhupada would often chant 12 rounds. Sometimes he would chant more.[/quote]
Where is the evidence of this cdd? more heresay?
user [252] · 2008-06-08
There are levels on which the 4 regulations can be followed, and there are levels of purity of chanting. So the best thing is to always depend on the causeless mercy of Lord Caitanya for ones deliverance (Ahaituki Kripa), rather than depending on the strength of ones chanting and strength in following the regulations.
user [166] · 2008-06-08
[quote][cite] anonymvisitor:[/cite]There are levels on which the 4 regulations can be followed, and there are levels of purity of chanting. So the best thing is to always depend on the causeless mercy of Lord Caitanya for ones deliverance (Ahaituki Kripa), rather than depending on the strength of ones chanting and strength in following the regulations.[/quote]
Its a combination.....ones endeavour to follow the process in a serious way and our dependance on the mercy of the Lord....according to the endeavour of the devotee the Lord awards that endeavour accordingly....the strength you speak of comes from the Lord and not just our own endeavour (although that has to be there).
user [154] · 2008-06-09
[quote][cite] rasa108:[/cite][quote][cite] ccd:[/cite]Prabhupada would often chant 12 rounds. Sometimes he would chant more.[/quote]
Where is the evidence of this cdd? more heresay?[/quote] Its a well measured timing of his rounds that he did very early in the morning. Is it wrong?
user [166] · 2008-06-09
[quote][cite] ccd:[/cite][quote][cite] rasa108:[/cite][quote][cite] ccd:[/cite]Prabhupada would often chant 12 rounds. Sometimes he would chant more.[/quote]
Where is the evidence of this cdd? more heresay?[/quote] Its a well measured timing of his rounds that he did very early in the morning. Is it wrong?[/quote]

Where is the evidence that SP only chanted 12 rounds daily?
user [154] · 2008-06-09
[quote][cite] rasa108:[/cite][quote][cite] ccd:[/cite][quote][cite] rasa108:[/cite][quote][cite] ccd:[/cite]Prabhupada would often chant 12 rounds. Sometimes he would chant more.[/quote]
Where is the evidence of this cdd? more heresay?[/quote] Its a well measured timing of his rounds that he did very early in the morning. Is it wrong?[/quote]

Where is the evidence that SP only chanted 12 rounds daily?[/quote]
It is the time he spent chanting. Nobody said only, normally..
